root_motivation: Silent filter drops in MQO compilation are invisible to callers — agents and analysts have no way to know their year/member constraints were discarded and the result reflects all data rather than the filtered slice. The root cause is that the server binds filters best-effort and records nothing when one fails; this library makes every binding outcome explicit so the confusion between 'wrong path chosen' and 'right path, filter dropped' is finally attributable.
user_persona: AI agents calling query_multidimensional who receive an unfiltered total disguised as a filtered answer; data engineers who want dropped-filter telemetry to diagnose member-key format issues at the engine level; analysts who need assurance that a constraint like 'for 2001' actually constrained the result.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given an MQO with two filters where one binds and one does not, report_filters returns applied=[bound] and dropped=[unbound] each typed.
Given any MQO, applied ∪ dropped equals the input filter set with no duplicates or omissions. Property test.
Given the canonical '2001' year filter that the engine drops, it appears in dropped with reason UnbindableMember.
Given an unbindable member where the catalog defines the level's key shape, the dropped entry includes the expected member-key shape as a suggestion.
SQL string-matching fallback marks entries as heuristic confidence; exact compiler binding record marks entries as exact.
Malformed MQO or compiled query returns typed error or fully-dropped report — never panics.
cargo test passes; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; zero unsafe.

scope: Define BoundMqo and CompiledQuery as owned types matching the PRD interface
Partition every MQO filter into applied or dropped, no filter unaccounted for
Type dropped filters with DropReason (UnbindableMember, UnknownHierarchy, UnknownLevel, UnsupportedFilterType)
Mark detection confidence as exact (compiler binding record present) or heuristic (SQL string-match fallback)
Emit as additive filter_bind_report field on response (serde serializable)
Property-test the applied∪dropped==input invariant via proptest
non_goals: Fixing member-key binding in the AtScale engine
Validating semantic correctness of filters for the question being asked
Blocking queries because a filter was dropped (annotation only in v1)
Network or LLM calls of any kind

five_whys_trace.q: Why do agents report wrong answers on wrong_date_role tasks?
five_whys_trace.a: The agent applied a year filter, but the compiled query silently dropped it, returning all-years data instead of the 2001 slice.

five_whys_trace.q: Why was the filter silently dropped?
five_whys_trace.a: The engine binds filters best-effort during compilation; an unbindable member (wrong key format or level) is discarded with no record in the response.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does no record exist in the response?
five_whys_trace.a: The response surface only carries compiled_query SQL and rows; there is no filter-binding outcome field — the API was never designed to report what it bound.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does that matter for the benchmark scorer?
five_whys_trace.a: The scorer cannot distinguish 'agent chose wrong path' from 'agent chose right path but filter was silently discarded' — wrong_date_role measured at 50.0% path-mean when the true cause was filter drops.

five_whys_trace.q: Why fix it as a separate library rather than inside the engine?
five_whys_trace.a: The engine fix (OQ2) may be a server-side format bug requiring a different team; the library can be built independently right now, is additive (no behavior change to query execution), and gives agents and operators actionable signal regardless of whether the engine is patched.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does 'present in compiled query' mean SQL text search or a compiler binding record?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: NFR2 says detection must be based on the actual compiled query, not a re-implementation. If the CompiledQuery carries an explicit bound_filter_ids list, use it (exact confidence). If not, fall back to SQL text search of member keys (heuristic confidence). Both cases must be marked.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Are BoundMqo and CompiledQuery owned types or references to mqo-mcp-server internals?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Since mqo-mcp-server types are not published as a lib dep, define them as owned types in this crate matching the interface described in the PRD. The crate is the source of truth for the filter-bind-report API surface.
